clue: Straight entertainers with stuffing knocked out by a funny show
blog: A , SITCOM (funny show), E {ntertainer} S [with stuffing knocked out]. Liquor may be taken straight / as it comes / with nothing added.

clue: Split blades
blog: Two meanings, the first as  ‘inform on’

clue: Percussion ensemble with something to play, short track
blog: GAME (something to play), LAN {e} (track – path ) [short]. ODE: gamelan – a traditional instrumental ensemble in Java and Bali, including many bronze percussion instruments.

clue: Parallel eating habits originally related to nutrition
blog: TROP ⁁I C (parallel) containing [eating] H {abits} [originally]. Tropic – the parallel of latitude 23°26ʹ north (tropic of Cancer) or south (tropic of Capricorn) of the equator. Trophic – relating to feeding and nutrition – I don’t recall meeting this before.

clue: Beat time, having raised orchestral baton first of all
blog: T {ime} + H {aving} + R {aised} + O {rchestral} + B {aton} [first of all]

clue: I go in nest, after flying about, for feeding
blog: Anagram [after flying about] of I GO IN NEST

clue: Sweet or savoury dish, mixed type
blog: Two meanings. I thought the second was to do with piebald horses or magpies being of two colours, but Chambers has simply: pie – confusedly mixed type (printing).

clue: Heavenly body gave interns breaks
blog: Anagram [breaks] of GAVE INTERNS

clue: One drink, Australian opening port, nothing special
blog: I (one) + MEAD (drink) + OZ (Australian) contained by [opening] AD ⁁ EN (port)

clue: Only a handful I’m relieved to hear?
blog: Aural wordplay [to hear]: “phew” (I’m relieved)

clue: Drink separately in fun
blog: TEA (drink), SINGLY (separately)

clue: Camel leaving river with antelope
blog: {Cam} EL (leaving river – R. Cam ), AND (with)

clue: Queen rejected by girl, possibly put out again?
blog: ER (Queen) reversed [rejected], ISSUE (girl, possibly – offspring generally )

clue: Demanding individual, one no longer taking a part?
blog: EX-ACTOR (one no longer taking a part)

clue: Raised mark on back of hand resided
blog: {han} D [back of…], WELT (raised mark)

clue: High jinks in Equus, perhaps
blog: HORSE PLAY (Equus, perhaps). Equus is a play by Peter Shaffer, about a psychiatrist who attempts to treat a young man who has a pathological religious fascination with horses.

clue: Answer that’s not filled in, understood in secret language
blog: A {nswe} R [that’s not filled in], GOT (understood)

clue: Dip very short tongue?
blog: 1 MM (very short – 1 millimetre ), ERSE (tongue)

clue: Suit hosting party concerned with vegetarian food
blog: CA ⁁ SE ( law suit) containing [hosting] LAB ( political party) + RE (concerned with)

clue: Newsworthy story best friend chewed over here?
blog: A cryptic hint referring to the adage that a man’s best friend is his dog. The definition refers to the idea that ‘dog bites man’ is no news whereas ‘man bites dog’ would be newsworthy.

clue: Sponge cakes go flat in the end
blog: {cake} S + {g} O + {fla} T [in the end]. Both are terms for a heavy drinker.

clue: I’m disgusted by 24 six-packs?
blog: Two meanings. 24 x 6 = 144 (a gross).

clue: Infidel witnessing robbery?
blog: AT HEIST (witnessing robbery)

clue: Part of scrum down, score after collapse
blog: Anagram [after collapse] of DOWN SCORE

clue: Beastly thing and gruesome display for the audience
blog: Aural wordplay [for the audience]: GRIZZLY / “grisly”(gruesome) + BEAR / “bare” (display – expose ). Edited.

clue: Lit up — as ceilings, perhaps?
blog: Two meanings

clue: Drink large rum and energy drink
blog: GIN (drink), anagram [rum] of LARGE , then E (energy)

clue: Picture this writer and soldier in shock
blog: I (t his writer), then GI (soldier) contained by [in] MA ⁁ NE (shock – hair )

clue: Complex pattern generated by craft adrift on American lake
blog: Anagram [adrift] of CRAFT , then A (American), L (lake). Collins: In geometry, a fractal is a shape made up of parts that are the same shape as itself and are of smaller and smaller sizes. Not a word I knew. It has made two previous appearances here, 15 and 16 years ago when I also didn’t know it.

clue: Introduce class who are popular?
blog: IN (popular), SET (class)

clue: Hat raised, lifted with courtesy, ultimately
blog: BRED (raised) reversed [lifted], {courtes} Y [ultimately]. American for a bowler hat.

clue: Old letter delivered in time, thankfully
blog: Hidden [delivered in] {tim} E TH {ankfully}. A character in the runic alphabet aka ‘edh’.
